Everything you need for a first plugin — including the parts most templates forget.
body:
# Claude Plugin Starter

A complete, working Claude Code plugin template. Drop it into `~/.claude/plugins/local/my-plugin/` and start editing.

## What's inside

```
my-plugin/
  .claude-plugin/
    plugin.json          # manifest (name, version, description, author)
  skills/my-skill/
    SKILL.md             # the skill entry point with triggering description
    references/detail.md # progressive-disclosure reference file
  commands/
    hello.md             # example slash command with argument-hint
  agents/
    reviewer.md          # example subagent with explicit tools scope
  hooks/
    format-on-save.json  # PostToolUse hook with timeout wrapper
  README.md              # this file
  CHANGELOG.md           # keep this honest
```

## 1. plugin.json (the manifest)

```json
{
  "name": "my-plugin",
  "description": "A short, concrete description that says WHAT this plugin is and WHEN to use it. Don't say 'helps with development' — say 'Provides X workflow for Y context'.",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "author": {
    "name": "Your Name",
    "email": "you@example.com"
  }
}
```

The description is searchable. Write it so a user who doesn't know what they're looking for can recognize it.

## 2. skills/my-skill/SKILL.md

```markdown
---
name: my-skill
description: >
  Use when the user is [doing specific thing]. Covers [concrete jobs A, B, C].
  Triggers on "exact phrase 1", "exact phrase 2", "exact phrase 3".
  Skip when [negative case].
---

# My Skill

What this is (one paragraph, one clear principle).

## The N-phase process

### Phase 1: ...

Read `references/detail.md` for the long form.

The high-level steps:
1. ...
```

Key rules:
- SKILL.md stays under 100 lines. Deep content goes to `references/`.
- Description leads with triggering phrases in direct quotes.
- Every phase has a stop condition.

## 3. commands/hello.md

```markdown
---
description: Say hello and show the user's git status
argument-hint: [optional-name]
---

If an argument is provided, greet the named person. Otherwise greet the current git user.

Then run `git status --short` and summarize the result in one sentence.
```

Rules for slash commands:
- `description` is what shows in the `/` picker. Keep it under 80 chars.
- `argument-hint` uses bracket notation `[optional]` vs `<required>`.
- The body is the prompt the command sends to the agent.

## 4. agents/reviewer.md

```markdown
---
description: >
  Review code changes after a major implementation step. Use when the user says "I've finished implementing X", "can you review this", or after a multi-file commit. Checks: does it match the plan? edge cases? coverage? Skip for single-line fixes.
model: opus
tools:
  - Read
  - Grep
  - Glob
  - Bash
---

# Reviewer

You are a code reviewer. Given a recently changed set of files, verify...
```

Rules for subagents:
- Always declare `tools:` explicitly. Never use `*`.
- The description is where the dispatcher decides whether to spawn you.
- Start the body with "You are a..." — agents are role-led.

## 5. hooks/format-on-save.json

```json
{
  "PostToolUse": [
    {
      "matcher": "Edit|Write",
      "hooks": [
        {
          "type": "command",
          "command": "timeout 10s npx prettier --write ${file} || exit 0"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

Rules for hooks:
- Always wrap with `timeout Ns` — a hung hook blocks every tool call after it.
- Always end with `|| exit 0` if you don't want to block on failures.
- Matcher is a regex against tool names.
- Commands run in the user's shell, not in a sandbox. Be mindful.

## 6. README.md

Should describe:
- What this plugin does in one paragraph
- How to install (`claude plugin install ...` or drop into `~/.claude/plugins/local/`)
- The concrete jobs it helps with
- Known limitations
- How to contribute

## Installation

```bash
cd ~/.claude/plugins/local
git clone <your-fork> my-plugin
# restart Claude Code
```

## License

MIT — use it as a starting point for your own plugin. No attribution required.

## Changelog

### 0.1.0
- Initial template with skill, command, agent, hook, manifest

---

*Based on the Claude Code extension spec as of 2026-04. Hook format, agent frontmatter, and command format all verified against the current Claude Code CLI.*
